student_input: I got 450/1100 in Matric, 400/1100 in FSc, and 30/200 in MDCAT. Can I get admission in King Edward Medical University?

state.student_input: I got 450/1100 in Matric, 400/1100 in FSc, and 30/200 in MDCAT. Can I get admission in King Edward Medical University?
state.advice: I understand that you're disappointed about not meeting the eligibility cutoff for King Edward Medical University, but I want to acknowledge that your calculated merit score of 26.14% is a significant achievement. While it's true that you don't meet the eligibility criteria, it's essential to focus on the areas where you can improve. Your performance in the MDCAT is a good starting point, and you can build upon that. I would recommend taking a closer look at your previous academic performance, particularly in your matric and FSC exams, where you scored 450 out of 1100 and 400 out of 1100 respectively. Identifying your strengths and weaknesses will help you create a more effective plan to improve your eligibility status. Additionally, exploring alternative universities or programs that may be a better fit for your academic abilities and interests can be a viable option. It's also crucial to remember that this is just a setback, and with hard work and dedication, you can achieve your academic goals. I encourage you to use this experience as an opportunity to learn and grow, and to work towards improving your academic performance for future applications.
